title: Anchor hover variants are malformed, so linked badges do not get the intended hover styles
reasoning: The badge variants use Tailwind arbitrary variants like `[a]:hover:bg-primary/80`, but the component supports rendering as another element via `render`, and these classes appear intended to style the badge when it is rendered as an anchor. Tailwind arbitrary selectors for the element itself need an `&` placeholder, for example `[a&]:hover:bg-primary/80`; `[a]` instead describes a selector/attribute-style variant that does not target the badge element as an anchor. As a result, anchor-rendered badges can keep base colors on hover instead of the variant-specific hover feedback.
reproduction: Render `<Badge render={<a href="#" />}>Link</Badge>` and inspect the generated hover selector/classes; the intended anchor self-selector is absent, so the default/secondary/destructive/outline anchor hover styles do not apply to the anchor badge itself.
recommendation: Change the anchor-specific variants from `[a]:hover:...` to a self-selector form such as `[a&]:hover:...` for each affected variant, matching the render-as-anchor behavior.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were linked for this component, and there is no visual or class-generation coverage asserting anchor-rendered badge hover behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component/unit snapshot or DOM class assertion for `Badge` rendered as an anchor, covering at least `default` and `outline`, and assert the generated class list contains the corrected `[a&]:hover:*` variants.
minimumFixScope: Update `frontend/src/components/ui/badge.tsx` variant class strings for the anchor-specific hover selectors.
